OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND STAY ENFORCEMENT OF FOREIGN MONEY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE CADLE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KIM MICHAEL GALE'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND STAY ENFORCEMENT OF FOREIGN MONEY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE JUDGMENT IS ENFORCEABLE. THE DECLARATION OF KNOLES FAILS TO SET FORTH AN ADEQUATE FOUNDATION FOR HIS BASIS TO AUTHENTICATE EXHIBITS A - E. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A MATERIAL DISPUTE OF FACT AS TO EXHIBIT B AND D HAS FAILED TO MEET THE BEST EVIDENCE REQUIREMENT OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1520, IN PARTICULAR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO OBTAIN A CERTIFIED COPY OF THE DOCUMENT FROM THE TEXAS COURT. BECAUSE B IS NOT ADMISSIBLE, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS AN ENFORCEABLE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS GRANTED AND DEFENDANT'S MOTION OF SEPTEMBER 10, 2007 TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND STAY ENFORCEMENT OF FOREIGN JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KIM MICHAEL GALE'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND STAY ENFORCEMENT OF FOREIGN MONEY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: James R. Knoles, Esq. of Brewer & Brewer 714/424-6300 for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); Derek A. Eletich, Esq. of Law Office of Derek A. Eletich 650/543-5477 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE JUDGMENT IS ENFORCEABLE. THE DECLARATION OF KNOLES FAILS TO SET FORTH AN ADEQUATE FOUNDATION FOR HIS BASIS TO AUTHENTICATE EXHIBITS A - E. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A MATERIAL DISPUTE OF FACT AS TO EXHIBIT B AND D HAS FAILED TO MEET THE BEST EVIDENCE REQUIREMENT OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1520, IN PARTICULAR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO OBTAIN A CERTIFIED COPY OF THE DOCUMENT FROM THE TEXAS COURT. BECAUSE B IS NOT ADMISSIBLE, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS AN ENFORCEABLE JUDGMENT. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/MW-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
